checks.result: ok, all packages green (engine 4.993s, btree 39.269s, catalog 132.251s, graph 2.851s, mvcc 50.385s, split 120.194s, wal 5.627s; loadtest/rpc no test files)

environmental_note: A first run of `go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 25m` (mid-session, machine load average ~20-36 from heavy concurrent activity on this shared dev box) hit btree package's own -test.timeout=25m0s and FAILED with a goroutine-dump timeout inside TestConcurrentMixedWorkload (1502.444s elapsed). engine/btree was NOT touched by this fix (confirmed via `git diff --stat -- btree/` = empty). Re-ran `go test ./btree/... -count=1 -timeout 15m` in isolation immediately after: PASS in 16.164s (well under budget), confirming the timeout was pure CPU-contention/environmental, not a regression. Re-ran the full `go test ./... -count=1 -timeout 25m` a second time once load settled: all packages PASS, including btree at 39.269s (matching the ~38.858s reference from the original 034-implementation run almost exactly). Final self-consistency result above reflects this clean second run.

teeth_confirmation.method: Temporarily reintroduced a non-atomic (truncate + partial write + 5ms sleep + partial write, no rename) writeNewContentFile in engine/split/execute.go, then ran `go test ./split/... -race -run TestReaderDuringSplit -count=5 -timeout 5m`.
teeth_confirmation.result: FAILED 5/5, each with an explicit torn-content assertion (e.g. observed truncated 'HIVEMIND-REDIR' mid-write, or empty string), proving the rewritten test genuinely detects a real torn-read regression.
teeth_confirmation.revert: execute.go restored to its exact pre-injection (commit 3e95aa2) state via `cp` from a pre-injection backup; confirmed `go vet`/`gofmt` clean and `go test ./split/... -race -count=1 -timeout 20m` green afterward.
